earth-center.wrongBelief: [UR] Earth is the center of the universe
earth-center.whySense: [UR] Everything appears to move around us. The sun rises and sets.
earth-center.overturned: [UR] Copernicus (1543) proposed heliocentrism. Galileo observed Jupiter's moons.
earth-center.indianConnection: [UR] Aryabhata (499 CE) proposed Earth rotates on its axis — over 1000 years before Copernicus.

flat-earth.whySense: [UR] The ground looks flat. The horizon looks like a straight line.
flat-earth.overturned: [UR] Pythagoras (500 BCE) reasoned Earth must be spherical.

four-elements.wrongBelief: [UR] Everything is made of earth, water, fire, and air

bloodletting.wrongBelief: [UR] Disease is caused by an imbalance of four humors

bloodletting.overturned: [UR] Germ theory (Pasteur, 1860s) showed diseases are caused by microorganisms.
bloodletting.indianConnection: [UR] Ayurveda uses the Tridosha system (Vata, Pitta, Kapha) emphasizing holistic balance and diet rather than mere bloodletting.

messages.text: [UR] Hey Infinity. Everyone thinks I am nothing. But without me, math falls apart.
[UR] I know the feeling. They think I am a number. I'm actually a direction, an endless journey.
[UR] True. If someone tries to divide by me, I break the universe.
[UR] And if they try to add 1 to me, nothing changes. We are the ultimate rule-breakers.
[UR] Indian mathematicians gave me a symbol — a dot, then a circle (shunya). What about you?
[UR] John Wallis gave me this ∞ shape in 1655. But the idea of endlessness is ancient.
[UR] I bring things together. I build stars, planets, and galaxies.
[UR] And I tear them apart. I am the reason things decay and spread out.
[UR] We are complete opposites. I create order out of chaos.
[UR] But in the end, I always win. The universe is moving toward maximum chaos.
[UR] Unless... a black hole? I crush everything into a singularity.
[UR] Even black holes evaporate over time (Hawking radiation). Told you I win.
[UR] I am the master blueprint. I hold the code for life safely in the nucleus.
[UR] Yeah, but you never leave the nucleus. You're just a massive, secure hard drive.
[UR] Security is important! I'm a stable double helix.
[UR] I'm the one who actually gets things done. I read your code, go to the ribosomes, and build the proteins.
[UR] True, you are the messenger. Without you, I'm just static information.
[UR] Exactly. You're the architect, but I'm the construction worker.

gravity: [UR] Why does the Moon orbit the Earth but an apple falls straight down, just like Aryabhata wondered?
[UR] If gravity pulls everything, why don't clouds fall?
[UR] If you dropped a rock into a hole through the center of the Earth, where would it go?
[UR] How does gravity help keep the oceans in place?
photosynthesis: [UR] If a Banyan tree eats sunlight and water, where does the heavy wood come from?
[UR] Could humans ever learn to photosynthesize like plants?
[UR] What happens to the oxygen after the plant breathes it out?
[UR] If a plant is kept in the dark, how long can it survive on stored food?
fractions: [UR] If we share a stuffed paratha, is 1/2 bigger than 1/3?
[UR] How can we divide 3 parathas equally among 4 friends?
[UR] If you cut a cake into infinite pieces, what is the size of each piece?
[UR] Why is dividing by a fraction the same as multiplying by its flipped version?
magnetism: [UR] If a magnet can pull iron, why can't it pull a gold coin?
[UR] How do birds use the Earth's magnetic field to navigate?
[UR] What would happen if the Earth lost its magnetic field?
[UR] Can you create a magnet using just electricity?
water-cycle: [UR] Is the water in the Ganges the exact same water the dinosaurs drank?
[UR] How does water escape the ocean and float into the sky as a cloud?
[UR] Where does the water go when a puddle dries up?
[UR] If all the ice on Earth melted, where would the water go?

values.title: [UR] Wonder
values.desc: [UR] We begin with questions, not answers. Every concept starts with a mystery, not a definition. We protect the natural curiosity every child is born with.

values.title: [UR] Equity
values.desc: [UR] One platform. For everyone. The child in a village in Odisha and the child in an apartment in Bengaluru should see the same beautiful concept, the same simulation, the same joy.

values.title: [UR] Respect
values.desc: [UR] No grades, no judgment, no shame. Every learner is equal. The 6-year-old discovering colours. The 40-year-old who always wanted to understand gravity. Both are welcome here.

values.title: [UR] Patience
values.desc: [UR] Learning takes the time it takes. No countdown timers on understanding. No pressure to move faster. The concept waits. Always.

values.title: [UR] Joy
values.desc: [UR] If learning is not joyful, something is wrong with the design — not with the learner. Science is joyful. Mathematics is joyful. Discovery is joyful.

values.title: [UR] Identity
values.desc: [UR] Indian scientists, Indian examples, Indian languages, Indian realities. This is not a global platform translated for India. It is built for India, from India.

difference_points.title: [UR] Free by Design
difference_points.desc: [UR] There is no 'poor version' and 'rich version' of learning on Jigyasu. Everything is free. Forever. For everyone. This is not a feature. It is a principle.

difference_points.title: [UR] Website First
difference_points.desc: [UR] A link shared through WhatsApp can reach a child in a village. No installation, no storage pressure, no app store barrier. Just open and learn.

difference_points.title: [UR] Mother Tongue
difference_points.desc: [UR] Thinking happens in our mother tongue. All 22 official scheduled Indian languages are fully supported, ensuring language is never a barrier to understanding.

difference_points.title: [UR] Playful Simulations
difference_points.desc: [UR] We don't just tell. We let you play. Interactive physics engines, math visualizers, and biology sandboxes turn abstract ideas into toys you can touch.

ocean-explorers.name: [UR] Ocean Explorers
ocean-explorers.description: [UR] Dive into a week of ocean-themed adventures! Create sea creatures, explore water science, and learn about marine life.

day1.title: [UR] Paper Plate Fish
day1.description: [UR] Start your ocean adventure by creating colorful tropical fish from paper plates!

day2.title: [UR] Underwater Volcano
day2.description: [UR] Create an underwater volcanic eruption! Learn about chemical reactions while making ocean-themed lava.

day3.title: [UR] Rainstick Waves
day3.description: [UR] Build a rainstick that sounds like ocean waves! Perfect for relaxation and music.

day4.title: [UR] Beach Treasure Hunt
day4.description: [UR] Go on a beach-themed scavenger hunt! Find shells, smooth rocks, and ocean treasures.

day5.title: [UR] Ocean Diorama Showcase
day5.description: [UR] Combine everything you've learned to create an ocean diorama featuring all your sea creatures!

space-mission.name: [UR] Space Mission
space-mission.description: [UR] Blast off into a galaxy of space exploration! Build rockets, learn about planets, and discover the mysteries of the universe.

day1.title: [UR] Paper Rocket Ship
day1.description: [UR] Design and build your own rocket ship! Learn about aerodynamics and space travel.

day2.title: [UR] Balloon Rocket Race
day2.description: [UR] Launch balloon rockets across the room! Learn about thrust and Newton's third law.

day3.title: [UR] Galaxy Painting
day3.description: [UR] Create stunning galaxy art with salt painting technique! Make stars sparkle and nebulas swirl.

day4.title: [UR] Stargazing Night
day4.description: [UR] Explore the real night sky! Find constellations and learn about distant stars and planets.

day5.title: [UR] Alien Puppet Show
day5.description: [UR] Create alien sock puppets and put on a space adventure puppet show! Use your imagination to tell stories from the cosmos.

backyard-safari.name: [UR] Backyard Safari
backyard-safari.description: [UR] Embark on a wild adventure in your own backyard! Discover insects, plants, and the fascinating ecosystem right outside your door.

day1.title: [UR] Bug Hotel Construction
day1.description: [UR] Build a cozy home for beneficial insects! Learn about the bugs that help our gardens.

day2.title: [UR] Dancing Raisins Insects
day2.description: [UR] Watch "bugs" dance up and down in soda! Learn about buoyancy and gases.

day3.title: [UR] Animal Masks
day3.description: [UR] Create masks of your favorite safari animals! Transform into lions, elephants, or butterflies.

day4.title: [UR] Nature Scavenger Hunt
day4.description: [UR] Explore your backyard safari! Find insects, plants, and animal tracks.

day5.title: [UR] Safari Binoculars & Field Guide
day5.description: [UR] Create your own safari binoculars and nature field guide! Document all the creatures you discovered this week.

maker-week.name: [UR] Maker Week
maker-week.description: [UR] Put on your engineering hat! Build, experiment, and create amazing contraptions through hands-on making.

day1.title: [UR] Rubber Band Car
day1.description: [UR] Build a car powered by rubber band energy! Learn about stored energy and motion.

day2.title: [UR] Catapult Challenge
day2.description: [UR] Construct a working catapult! Learn about levers and launch pompoms across the room.

day3.title: [UR] Egg Drop Engineering
day3.description: [UR] Design a device to protect an egg from a high drop! Apply engineering principles to solve real problems.

day4.title: [UR] Marble Run
day4.description: [UR] Engineer an epic marble run with twists, turns, and drops! Experiment with gravity and momentum.

day5.title: [UR] Invention Convention
day5.description: [UR] Showcase all your inventions! Create a new robot or contraption that combines what you've learned.

privacy_intro_text: [UR] Welcome to Jigyasu. We are committed to protecting your privacy and ensuring a safe educational environment for children. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and protect information across our platform. Our privacy practices are designed to align with the principles of the Children's Online Privacy Protection Act (COPPA) and the Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDP Act).
privacy_collection_text: [UR] We only collect the absolute minimum information necessary to provide our educational service:
privacy_profile_data_text: [UR] Names, avatars, and learning progress are stored locally on your device. We do not transmit this data to our servers.
privacy_analytics_text: [UR] We use privacy-first, cookieless analytics (Plausible) to understand general usage patterns. No personally identifiable information (PII) is tracked or stored.
privacy_parent_rights_text: [UR] We respect the rights of parents and guardians under child privacy laws. Parents have the right to:
privacy_right_review: [UR] Review any information we have collected from their child.
privacy_right_refuse: [UR] Refuse further collection or use of their child's information.
privacy_third_parties_text: [UR] We do not sell data to third parties, nor do we run advertisements. All third-party services we use (such as for basic site hosting and analytics) are strictly evaluated for their privacy compliance and are not permitted to use our users' data for their own purposes.
privacy_retention_text: [UR] Because learning data is stored locally on your device, it is retained until you clear your browser's local storage or explicitly delete the profile within the app. Anonymous aggregate analytics data is kept indefinitely to help us improve the platform.
privacy_contact_text: [UR] If you have any questions or concerns about this Privacy Policy, please contact us at privacy@jigyasu.app.
terms_acceptance_text: [UR] By accessing or using Jigyasu, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our platform.
terms_acceptable_use_text: [UR] Jigyasu is an educational platform designed for children and adults. Users agree to:
terms_use_1: [UR] Use the platform solely for educational, non-commercial purposes.
terms_use_2: [UR] Not attempt to interfere with or disrupt the service or servers.
terms_use_3: [UR] Not use the platform in any way that violates applicable laws or regulations.
terms_liability_text: [UR] To the fullest extent permitted by law, Jigyasu shall not be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages resulting from your use of or inability to use the service.
terms_termination_text: [UR] We reserve the right to modify or discontinue the service at any time without notice. We may also terminate or suspend your access to the service immediately, without prior notice or liability, for any reason whatsoever, including without limitation if you breach the Terms.
terms_governing_law_text: [UR] These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of India, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.
terms_contact_text: [UR] For any questions about these Terms, please contact us at legal@jigyasu.app.

worlds.biology.modules.cell-map.wonder.m: [UR] What runs the microscopic city inside your body?
[UR] Every single cell in your body is like a bustling metropolis, complete with power plants, post offices, and recycling centers. Let's zoom in a million times and see how it works!
worlds.biology.modules.cell-map.wonder.e: [UR] Navigate through the Cell City. Tap on the different organelles to discover their jobs.
[UR] Find the 'Power Plant' of the cell. What is it called?
[UR] Which organelle acts like a post office, packaging proteins?
[UR] Compare a plant cell to an animal cell. What's the biggest difference?
worlds.biology.modules.cell-map.wonder.i: [UR] You've discovered the hidden machinery of life: Organelles!
[UR] Just like a city needs different workers to survive, your cells rely on these tiny structures to keep you alive, breathing, and moving.
worlds.biology.modules.cell-map.wonder.a: [UR] Without mitochondria producing energy, you wouldn't even be able to blink.
[UR] Think of the Golgi Apparatus like the massive Dabbawala network in Mumbai—sorting and delivering packages (proteins) exactly where they need to go with incredible precision!
[UR] Next time you eat an apple, remember you are consuming millions of tiny plant cell walls.

worlds.physics.modules.black-hole.wonder: [UR] What happens when gravity becomes so strong that not even light can escape?
[UR] Somewhere in the universe, massive stars collapse into infinitely dense points. These invisible giants shape galaxies. Let's safely approach the event horizon.
[UR] Adjust the mass of the black hole and observe how it warps the spacetime grid around it.
[UR] What happens to the fabric of spacetime as mass increases?
[UR] Try sending a light beam past the event horizon. What occurs?
[UR] Watch the time dilation meter as you get closer to the center.
[UR] You've witnessed Spacetime Curvature and the Event Horizon!
[UR] Mass tells spacetime how to curve, and spacetime tells mass how to move. A black hole is just an extreme valley in this fabric.
